rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks for component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Implementation-level AGREE bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections are defined.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should not reference subcomponent ports directly; use valid variables or outputs instead.
When placing AGREE annex blocks, ensure the scope matches the level of the component (type vs. implementation).
Preserve AADL architecture integrity when inserting AGREE annex blocks by aligning with component structure.
Use valid AADL scoping rules to ensure AGREE contracts are correctly associated with component types or implementations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type for port-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation for binding-level details.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level AGREE bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
